vulnerability.title: Disable SSH Support for .rhosts Files
vulnerability.description: SSH can emulate the behavior of the obsolete rsh command in allowing users to enable insecure access to their accounts via .rhosts files. The default SSH configuration disables support for .rhosts. The appropriate configuration is used if no value is set for IgnoreRhosts. To explicitly disable support for .rhosts files, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: IgnoreRhosts yes
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreRhosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreRhosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreRhosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complia

triage_decision.reason: Disabling .rhosts support is a standard SSH hardening practice that mitigates an obsolete and insecure authentication method. The change involves adding a configuration directive to sshd_config, which does not impact modern SSH key-based or password-based authentication.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential loss of access for legacy systems relying on .rhosts (highly unlikely in modern environments)

per_turn.command: ls -la /etc/ssh 2>/dev/null || ls -la /etc/ssh 2>&1 | sed 's/You are required to change your password immediately (password expired).//g; s/WARNING: Your password has expired.//g; s/Password change required but no TTY available.//g'

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan effectively addresses the vulnerability by explicitly setting 'IgnoreRhosts yes' in a configuration file within the sshd_config.d directory, which follows modern SSH configuration patterns and ensures the setting is enforced. The plan is concise and correct.
concerns: Ensure that the main /etc/ssh/sshd_config includes the line 'Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf', otherwise the new file will be ignored by the sshd daemon.
suggested_improvements: Verify the existence of the 'Include' directive in /etc/ssh/sshd_config before proceeding.
Run 'sshd -t' to validate the configuration syntax before restarting the service to prevent accidental lockout.
